query:
SELECT af.id, af.feedbackType, af.explanation, af.status, af.createdAt, af.callId, af.callGradeId, u.name as userName
FROM ai_feedback af
LEFT JOIN users u ON af.userId = u.id
WHERE af.status = 'pending'
ORDER BY af.createdAt DESC
LIMIT 20;

FROM ai_feedback af
LEFT JOIN users u ON af.userId = u.id
WHERE af.status = 'pending'
ORDER BY af.createdAt DESC
LIMIT 20;

rows.explanation: Daniel could have more explicitly set expectations at the beginning of the call, detailing the call structure and comfort with saying 'not a good fit', rather than just 'i just have a couple of questions to see if we're a good fit to work together'. same expectation, different wording
No use in qualifying thoroughly, manufactured home fully remodeled and was going to list, not in buybox
I already had notes from previous conversation on his number, the texting leads sometimes come in with more information
We would not be anywhere near his number and no motivation whatsoever

messages: 240002	general_correction	Daniel should have explicitly set expectations at the beginning of the call, stating the call structure and comfort with saying 'not a good fit.' He missed the opportunity after 'Am I catching you at a good time? Yeah, you're fine.'
to extract more pain and urgency.
After the seller stated their cash price of '$490' and their listing price of '$530' with a realtor, to gauge their flexibility.

Daniel prematurely disqualified the lead based on the property's condition and recent purchase without fully exploring the seller's motivation or willingness to consider a discounted cash offer.
